title: 9 Contingency Procedures for Returning Cheques
body_text:
9	Contingency Procedures for Returning Cheques
9.1	Contingency Procedures for Returning Cheques
If cheque returns canât be entered in ORS, and your financial institution is unable to access ServiceNow, Central 1 will process the cheque returns on behalf of your financial institution.
If your financial institution can make the pay/no pay decision, but doesnât have access to the cheque image (for example, your financial institution doesnât have access to ORS, but can make the decision using a settlement report), proceed as follows:
Complete the Contingency Cheque Returns, Form 2304. See Figure 1 and Figure 2. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Figure 1: Contingency Cheque Returns, Form 2304, Page 1
Figure 2: Contingency Cheque Returns, Form 2304, Page 2
Fax or email a copy of the form, along with any supporting documentation (such as settlement reports) to:
Tracers
Fax:
604-730-6460
Phone:
604-730-6405
Email:
tracers1@central1.com
If your financial institution is unable to make the pay/no pay decision because they donât have access to the cheque image (for example, your financial institution needs to verify the signature on a large value cheque), your financial institution can request a cheque image from Central 1âs Tracing Department.
Cheque returns submitted in a contingency situation are processed on a best-efforts basis and may be subject to additional fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Related Information
Contingency Cheque Returns,
Form 2304
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
END
